mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, offering significantly improved performance and resource efficiency. It is ideal for applications requiring high throughput and low latency caching. Key trade-offs include the need for compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached clients and potential learning curve for advanced features.

steps.title: Add Dragonfly Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dragonfly using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflydb.github.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Dragonfly Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dragonfly to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Dragonfly.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dragonfly application in the specified namespace using Helm, ensuring you are using the correct version.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Installs Dragonfly in the 'dragonfly' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Dragonfly Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Dragonfly custom resource to deploy an instance of Dragonfly in your cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: example-dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: example-dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly is Running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dragonfly pods to ensure that the installation was successful and that the application is running.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> This command lists all pods in the 'dragonfly' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dragonfly application and its associated resources from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in Dragonfly, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> Uninstalls the Dragonfly release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step involves draining the pods, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of the Dragonfly pod to be drained.
> Upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating custom resource: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRD for Dragonfly has not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Dragonfly pod is failing to start due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dragonfly and correct any configuration errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Verify your network connectivity and ensure that the Kubernetes API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind 'Dragonfly' in version 'dragonflydb.com/v1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Dragonfly CRD has not been registered in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure the CRD is installed correctly and check the CRD status with: kubectl get crd

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Redis commands, improved memory efficiency, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly pods in the 'Running' state when executing: kubectl get pods -n dragonfly.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: example-dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
